brief_document.title: aion-worker-typescript: packaging, protocol session, loop, and reconnect
blocked_by: aion-proto gRPC/protobuf definitions (cluster AW) must be available to generate TypeScript proto types from
brief_document.purpose: Bring the worker protocol to the Node ecosystem. Port the reference state machine (AR-001..AR-006) to TypeScript: an npm package with a session over @grpc/grpc-js, a concurrent worker loop, and reconnect-with-re-report. The author-facing typed-activity surface lands in AR-010; this brief is the protocol substrate.
brief_document.task: Create the aion-worker-typescript package (package.json with ESM+CJS builds and bundled type declarations, strict tsconfig) depending on @grpc/grpc-js, with proto types generated from aion-proto. Implement session.ts (connect+handshake+register behind a session interface), loop.ts (receive→dispatch→report with operator-configured bounded concurrency and backpressure), and reconnect.ts (backoff reconnect, re-register, re-report un-acked results). Mirror the Rust/Python semantics exactly. The dispatch seam calls into the typed-activity layer from AR-010.

requirements.title: npm packaging and proto generation
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define an npm package with strict TypeScript config, ESM and CJS builds, bundled type declarations, depending on @grpc/grpc-js, with proto types generated from aion-proto. index.ts SHALL contain only re-exports.
acceptance: package.json declares the package, the @grpc/grpc-js dependency, an ESM+CJS build (exports map), and a build step generating proto types into src/proto/.
tsconfig.json enables strict mode (strict: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess, no implicit any).
src/index.ts contains only re-exports of the public surface (no logic), per CO3.
The package builds (tsc / bundler) producing both ESM and CJS outputs plus .d.ts declarations; a smoke test imports the built package and asserts the public names exist.

requirements.title: Protocol session over @grpc/grpc-js
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL implement a session that connects to the engine endpoint, performs the handshake (task queue + identity), and registers activity-type names, behind a session interface the loop depends on.
acceptance: session.ts defines a session interface and a @grpc/grpc-js-backed implementation with connect+handshake (taskQueue, identity), register(activityTypes), a receive-task async iterable, reportResult, reportFailure, and sendHeartbeat.
The loop and reconnect modules depend on the session interface (per D3) so a fake in-memory session can substitute in tests (CO6).
Config (endpoint, taskQueue, identity, maxConcurrency, credentials passthrough) is read from an explicit WorkerConfig type with no hardcoded concurrency default (CO10).
A test substitutes a fake session and asserts handshake and registration carry the configured task queue, identity, and activity names.

requirements.title: Worker loop with bounded concurrency
requirements.spec: WHILE connected, THE SYSTEM SHALL consume the receive stream, dispatch each task as a concurrent promise up to the configured max concurrency, and report completion or failure. THE SYSTEM SHALL apply backpressure when saturated and SHALL NOT dispatch unbounded.
acceptance: loop.ts drives the session's receive async iterable, dispatching each task to a seam (filled by AR-010) and reporting outcomes via the session.
Concurrency is bounded by maxConcurrency (a counting limiter); when saturated the loop stops pulling new tasks (backpressure), not buffering unboundedly.
A test sets maxConcurrency=2, feeds 5 slow tasks via the fake session, and asserts peak concurrent dispatch is 2.
Each received/reported task is logged via the package's logger without logging payload contents.

requirements.title: Reconnect and un-acked re-report
requirements.spec: WHEN the session drops, THE SYSTEM SHALL reconnect with bounded backoff, re-register, and re-report un-acked results before serving new tasks.
acceptance: reconnect.ts tracks reported-but-unacknowledged results keyed by activityId and reconnects with bounded exponential backoff (cap from config, not hardcoded).
On reconnect it re-runs handshake + registration and re-reports the un-acked backlog before the loop pulls any new task (per D7 / CO11).
A test reports a completion, simulates a disconnect before acknowledgement, reconnects, and asserts the completion is re-reported before any new task is dispatched.
The full reconnect path runs against the fake session with no live engine (CO6).

boundaries: SHALL NOT define defineActivity, the ctx, errors, or Worker class — that is AR-010 (the loop calls a dispatch seam AR-010 fills).
SHALL NOT define or alter aion-proto — it generates types from AW's definitions (CO5).
SHALL NOT implement engine-side de-duplication — that is AW.
verification: eslint passes with no disabled rules in new code (CO2).
vitest passes, including the concurrency-cap and reconnect-re-report tests.
The package builds ESM+CJS+.d.ts and imports cleanly; the protocol path is covered against the fake session.
No // @ts-ignore, // @ts-nocheck, or eslint-disable in new code.

checklist.text: aion-worker-typescript is an npm package (package.json with ESM+CJS builds and bundled type declarations) depending on @grpc/grpc-js, with index.ts exposing only re-exports and strict tsconfig.
A session module performs connect+handshake (task queue + identity) and activity-type registration over @grpc/grpc-js, behind a session abstraction the loop depends on.

A worker loop receives tasks from the server stream, dispatches up to the operator-configured concurrency as concurrent promises, and reports completion or failure; backpressure is applied when the pool is full.
The TypeScript SDK reconnects with bounded backoff, re-registers, and re-reports un-acked results before serving new tasks.

stories.text: As a TypeScript activity author, I want to install the SDK from npm with bundled type declarations so that it works out of the box in an ESM or CJS project.
As a worker operator, I want to configure the engine endpoint, task queue, identity, and max concurrency so that I can point a worker at the right engine and scale it without editing code.

As a worker operator, I want a worker to reconnect automatically after a network drop and re-report any work it already finished so that a disconnect never loses a completed activity or double-runs a side effect.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny in aion-worker (Rust). No unsafe.
No lint-bypass directives. Rust: no #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore]. Python: no blanket # type: ignore or # noqa. TS: no // @ts-ignore / // @ts-nocheck / eslint-disable. Fix the code instead.
mod.rs/lib.rs (Rust) and __init__.py/index.ts (Python/TS) are declarations and re-exports only — no logic.
500-line file limit (excluding tests/comments/whitespace) in every language.

The wire contract is owned by aion-proto (AW). This cluster references wire types and RPCs; it does not define or alter them. Where AW and this design disagree on a name or shape, AW wins and the SDK adapts.

The activity-execution machinery (loop, dispatch, context, classification, reconnect) must be testable without a live engine, against an in-memory fake session/endpoint. Tests needing a real engine use a runtime env-gate (AION_WORKER_TEST_URL) and skip with a logged line when unset.
Payload encode/decode uses the content-type tag from aion-core's Payload; JSON is the baseline codec present in all three SDKs. An SDK must not silently change content type on round-trip.

Failure classification (retryable vs terminal) is explicit at the author boundary in all three SDKs and maps onto the wire identically (D6). Unclassified-defaults-to-retryable is logged, never silent.
Cancellation is cooperative only (D5). No SDK forcibly kills a running handler's thread/task.
Concurrency limits are operator-configured (D9). No SDK hardcodes a concurrency cap or other tunable as an assumed default.
Reconnect must re-report unacknowledged results before serving new tasks (D7). Completed work is never dropped on a disconnect.

Per-language toolchains gate every brief: Rust cargo clippy -D warnings + cargo fmt --check + cargo test; Python ruff + mypy --strict + pytest; TS tsc --noEmit strict + eslint + vitest. All relevant gates pass clean before a brief lands.

resolved_context.intention: This cluster is how Tier 3 work — work that cannot run on beamr — gets done. A workflow dispatches an activity; a worker written in Python, TypeScript, or Rust, connected to the engine and holding a function registered under that activity's name, receives the task, runs it in its own runtime, and returns the result for the engine to record. On replay the recorded result comes back without re-execution. Three SDKs, three idioms (Python decorators + type hints + asyncio; TS generics + async/await + npm; Rust traits + async fn + cargo), one protocol, identical durability semantics. A workflow author does not know which language served an activity. It must feel native in each language while all three speak the exact same conversation with the engine, defined once in aion-proto.
